tree.setup: A neighborhood supermarket, fluorescent-lit and familiar. You're grabbing a few things for dinner. Auntie Qian works the checkout. She scans items at superhuman speed but always pauses to comment on what you're buying -- she has opinions about everyone's dinner choices.
tree.setup_zh: 社区超市，日光灯照着，很熟悉。你在买晚饭的东西。钱阿姨在收银台工作。她扫码速度超快，但总要停下来评论你买了什么——她对每个人的晚饭都有意见。

options.feedback: Auntie Qian scans your items while providing unsolicited dinner commentary.「鸡蛋不错。可是你没买菜啊！」就 means 'just' or 'only' here.

options.text_pinyin: děng yíxià, wǒ hái yào yí gè.
options.text_en: Wait, I need one more thing.
options.feedback: Auntie Qian waits with arms folded. She's seen this before -- the forgotten item dash. 还要 means 'also need.'
Auntie Qian points at the screen without pausing her scanning rhythm. The total is building.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一共三十二块。要袋子吗？
turns.text_pinyin: yígòng sānshí èr kuài. yào dàizi ma?
turns.text_en: Thirty-two yuan total. Do you need a bag?

options.feedback: Auntie Qian gives you an approving look -- she respects someone who comes prepared. She saves each unused bag in a drawer for reasons she's never explained.
She pulls one from under the counter.「加两毛钱。」Plastic bags cost extra now -- China's environmental policy at work.
「两毛钱。」Auntie Qian answers this fifty times a day. She could answer it in her sleep. She may have.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好了！回去做饭吧。别忘了买菜！
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo le! huíqù zuò fàn ba. bié wàng le mǎi cài!
turns.text_en: All done! Go make dinner. Don't forget to buy vegetables!

tree.cultural_note: Chinese supermarket cashiers (收银员) are famously fast and often chatty. Plastic bags now cost extra as part of environmental regulations. Bringing your own bag (自己带袋子) is increasingly common.
